title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA implements BCH ML bias-adjusted three-step estimators latent class analysis (LCA) covariates distal outcomes, following methodological framework BCH Vermunt’s ML approaches Bakk, Tekle & Vermunt (2013). tseLCA also builds top two-step LCA estimation procedure outlined Bakk & Kuha (2018), using R package multilevLCA efficient measurement model estimation Lyrvall et al. (2025). tseLCA provides analytic sandwich variance estimation propagates measurement uncertainty classification-error correction final step. three-step approach separates model : Step 1 — Estimate LCA measurement model (class-conditional item probabilities class prevalences). Step 2 — Assign posterior class probabilities compute misclassification matrix. Step 3 — Estimate structural model (covariate effects distal outcome means) using bias-adjusted weights.

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: built-data-generating process replicates design Bakk & Kuha (2018). dataset six binary indicators (Y1,…,Y6Y_1, \ldots, Y_6) drawn three-class LCA, plus either covariate Zp∼Uniform{1,…,5}Z_p \sim \text{Uniform}\{1,\ldots,5\} predicting class membership, continuous distal outcome ZoZ_o predicted class membership.
code: # High separation: P(Y_h = 1 | class) = 0.9 / 0.1 d <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "high",   scenario = "covariate",   seed = 1 ) head(d) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6 X Zp #> 1  1  1  1  0  0  0 2  1 #> 2  0  0  0  0  0  0 3  4 #> 3  1  0  1  0  0  0 2  1 #> 4  1  1  0  1  1  1 1  2 #> 5  0  0  0  0  0  0 3  5 #> 6  1  1  1  1  1  1 1  3 # Low separation: P(Y_h = 1 | class) = 0.7 / 0.3 # Zp and X are identical to 'd' because seed = 1 d.low <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "low",   scenario = "covariate",   seed = 1 ) head(d.low) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6 X Zp #> 1  1  1  1  0  0  0 2  1 #> 2  1  0  0  1  0  1 3  4 #> 3  0  0  1  0  0  0 2  1 #> 4  1  1  0  0  1  1 1  2 #> 5  0  0  1  1  1  0 3  5 #> 6  1  1  1  1  1  1 1  3

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: three_step() Zp.names Zo.name fits measurement model , returning tseLCA_measurement object. Internally calls multilevLCA::multiLCA() random restarts entropy R2R^2 low. low separation measurement model can struggle find global maximum. Use iter.measurement trigger number random restarts whenever entropy R2R^2 falls R2.threshold. plot() S3 method delegates multilevLCA’s item-profile plot.
code: d.measurement <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   measurement.tol = 1e-8 ) summary(d.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -1455.5052 #> AIC            : 2951.0104 #> BIC            : 3035.3025 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8780 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.3570 #> P(C2) 0.3308 #> P(C3) 0.3122 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.9237 0.8621 0.1187 #> P(Y2|C) 0.9083 0.9219 0.1178 #> P(Y3|C) 0.9148 0.9571 0.0731 #> P(Y4|C) 0.8843 0.1481 0.0875 #> P(Y5|C) 0.8817 0.1340 0.1118 #> P(Y6|C) 0.9174 0.0889 0.1252 d.low.measurement <- three_step(   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   iter.measurement = 10,   R2.threshold = 0.9 ) summary(d.low.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Log-likelihood : -2019.2458 #> AIC            : 4078.4916 #> BIC            : 4162.7837 #> Entropy R²     : 0.3327 #>  #> Class prevalences: #>              #> P(C1) 0.2753 #> P(C2) 0.4551 #> P(C3) 0.2696 #> attr(,"names") #> [1] "C1" "C2" "C3" #>  #> Item-response probabilities (P(Y=1|class)): #>             C1     C2     C3 #> P(Y1|C) 0.7328 0.6418 0.3345 #> P(Y2|C) 0.5723 0.7549 0.3223 #> P(Y3|C) 0.6937 0.7101 0.3036 #> P(Y4|C) 0.6846 0.4588 0.2105 #> P(Y5|C) 0.6947 0.4058 0.3787 #> P(Y6|C) 0.8651 0.3551 0.2456 plot(d.measurement)

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: fitZ_from_fit0() fixes measurement parameters Step-1 values estimates multinomial logit coefficients γ\gamma via EM. two-step estimates serve starting values Step 3 generally close final three-step estimates. Starting values high-separation fit can passed low-separation fit help converge.
code: d.fitZ <- fitZ_from_fit0(   fit0 = d.measurement$measurement_model$fit0,   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   Zp.names = "Zp" ) # True slopes: -1 (C2) and +1 (C3) relative to C1 d.fitZ$mGamma #>                   C2         C3 #> Intercept  2.1934130 -3.4524271 #> Zp        -0.9411383  0.8971774 d.low.fitZ <- fitZ_from_fit0(   fit0 = d.low.measurement$measurement_model$fit0,   data = d.low,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   Zp.names = "Zp",   starting_val = d.fitZ$mGamma ) d.low.fitZ$mGamma #>                   C2         C3 #> Intercept  3.0446368 -3.6948005 #> Zp        -0.9832597  0.9487391

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: single three_step() call handles three steps. default uses ML correction Vermunt (2010) modal class assignment. standard coef() vcov() S3 methods work tseLCA object.

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: modal assignment (use.modal.assignment = TRUE, default), Jacobian measurement-uncertainty correction mathematically defined. Setting use.modal.assignment = FALSE uses soft posterior weights throughout, giving analytic Jacobian recommended separation moderate low. use.modal.assignment = TRUE, Jacobian ∂θ2∂θ1\frac{\partial\theta_2}{\partial\theta_1} computed using full posterior weights (e.g., behaving use.modal.assignment = FALSE) maintain well-defined derivatives, though three-step estimates still computed modal assignment specified. different negligible separation high.
code: d.three_step.prop <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.modal.assignment = FALSE ) summary(d.three_step.prop) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -1339.0617 #> AIC            : 2758.1234 #> BIC            : 2926.7078 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8680  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  2.1934 -3.4524 #> Zp        -0.9411  0.8972 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   2.1096    0.3287  6.4183 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.9121    0.1460 -6.2456 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -3.6508    0.6430 -5.6777 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          0.9367    0.1547  6.0563 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: Setting use.simple.cov = TRUE skips measurement-uncertainty correction returns robust sandwich SEs Step 3 . separation high correction negligible, useful computational shortcut large samples.

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: BCH correction Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars (2004) available via use.bch = TRUE. works well high separation can produce ill-conditioned Hessian separation low (resulting covariance matrix positive semi-definite), case ML estimator preferred. BCH low-separation data can fail produce positive semi-definite Hessian. ML estimator proportional assignment reliable setting.
code: d.three_step.bch <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   use.bch = TRUE ) summary(d.three_step.bch) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : BCH #> Log-likelihood : -1339.2863 #> AIC            : 2758.5726 #> BIC            : 2927.1569 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8700  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  2.1934 -3.4524 #> Zp        -0.9411  0.8972 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   1.9554    0.3111  6.2844 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -0.8424    0.1304 -6.4613 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -3.4634    0.5697 -6.0790 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          0.8923    0.1385  6.4412 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 # title: tseLCA Workflow
text: default, class 1 ("C1") reference category multinomial logit parameterisation. rebase argument changes . Estimates reparameterised consistently: log-likelihoods invariant, coefficients satisfy transitivity relation log⁡(πt/πj)=log⁡(πt/π1)−log⁡(πj/π1)\log(\pi_t / \pi_j) = \log(\pi_t / \pi_1) - \log(\pi_j / \pi_1).

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: step1 argument accepts previously fitted tseLCA object raw output lca_step1(). useful want : Reuse expensive measurement model across multiple structural models. Estimate measurement model large reference sample apply smaller analysis sample. Inject custom two-step starting values computed via fitZ_from_fit0(). can also compute two-step starting values separately inject calling three_step().

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA uses two-pass row-filtering strategy matches multilevLCA’s approach measurement model allowing observations Steps 1 2 Step 3. incomplete = FALSE (default), row missing indicator dropped measurement model estimated. incomplete = TRUE, fully-missing rows dropped; partially observed rows contribute measurement model via FIML. Regardless incomplete, Step 3 drops row missing covariate. rows used Step 3 subset used Steps 1 2. FIML measurement model can passed reused covariate step sparse data.
code: set.seed(42) d.new <- generate_data(500, separation = "high", seed = 3) sparsity <- 0.1 missing <- 1 -   matrix(     rbinom(prod(dim(d.new)), size = 1, prob = sparsity),     nrow = nrow(d.new),     ncol = ncol(d.new)   ) missing[missing == 0] <- NA_real_ d.sparse <- d.new * missing head(d.sparse) #>   Y1 Y2 Y3 Y4 Y5 Y6  X Zp #> 1  0  0 NA  0  1  0  3  5 #> 2  1  1 NA  0  0  0  2  2 #> 3  1  1 n_classes = 3,   incomplete = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> 242 row(s) dropped from measurement/classification steps (missing Y). # Rows dropped = number of rows with at least one missing Y sum(apply(d.sparse[, paste0("Y", 1:6)], 1, \(x) any(is.na(x)))) #> [1] 242 summary(d.sparse.measurement) #> -- tseLCA Measurement Model -------------------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> title: tseLCA Workflow
text: tseLCA supports polytomous indicators, following multilevLCA’s convention item categories coded consecutive integers starting 0. reproduce example poLCA package.
code: data(election, package = "poLCA") elec <- election elec.items <- colnames(election)[1:12]  # Recode to 0-based integers as required by multilevLCA elec[, elec.items] <- lapply(elec[, elec.items], \(x) as.integer(x) - 1L) elec.measurement <- three_step(   data = elec,   Y.names = elec.items,   n_classes = 3,   #The poLCA example drops any row with a missing cell   incomplete = FALSE )  elec.three_step <- three_step(   data = elec,   Y.names = elec.items,   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = c("PARTY"),   step1 = elec.measurement$measurement_model,   incomplete = FALSE,   #With the neutral group as the base-category   rebase = "C3" ) #> Warning: lca_indiv_varmat: Infomat is singular even after removing boundary #> parameters; returning NA matrix. Check for near-empty classes. summary(elec.three_step) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -16278.0242 #> AIC            : 32852.0485 #> BIC            : 33617.2262 #> Entropy R²     : 0.7956  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C1      C2 #> Intercept -2.5781  1.8687 #> PARTY      0.4289 -0.6983 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value p.value #> Intercept:C1  -2.4701        NA      NA      NA #> PARTY:C1       0.4077        NA      NA      NA #> Intercept:C2   1.7324        NA      NA      NA #> PARTY:C2      -0.6727        NA      NA      NA #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 party.x <- seq(from = 1, to = 7, length.out = 101) pidmat <- cbind(1, party.x) exb <- exp(pidmat %*% coef(elec.three_step))  matplot(   party.x,   (cbind(1, exb)) / (1 + rowSums(exb)),   ylim = c(0, 1),   type = "l",   lwd = 3,   col = 1,   xlab = "Party ID: strong Democratic (1) to strong Republican (7)",   ylab = "Probability of latent class membership",   main = "Party ID as a predictor of candidate affinity class", ) text(3.9, 0.60, "Other") text(6.2, 0.6, "Bush affinity") text(2.0, 0.65, "Gore affinity")

title: tseLCA Workflow
text: distal outcomes (Zo←X→YZ_o \leftarrow X \rightarrow Y), supply Zo.name family argument. available families "gaussian" (default), "poisson", "binomial". ML BCH estimators available.
code: d.distal <- generate_data(   n = 500,   separation = "high",   scenario = "distal",   seed = 4 ) # True class means: mu = (0, 1, -1) for C1, C2, C3 d.distal.measurement <- three_step(   data = d.distal,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3 )  # title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA (Three-Step Estimation Latent Class Analysis) introduces bias-adjusted three-step estimation structural latent class models R. package provides comprehensive framework estimating latent class models covariates distal outcomes preserving measurement structure established class formation. Building upon efficient measurement-model estimation procedures implemented multilevLCA, tseLCA extends existing functionality modern three-step estimators, classification-error corrections, variance estimation procedures appropriately account uncertainty latent class measurement stage.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA first R package provide unified implementation modern bias-adjusted three-step estimators latent class analysis. contrast traditional one-step approaches (implemented popular package, poLCA), inclusion covariates may alter underlying latent class definitions, three-step methods preserve measurement model estimated first stage subsequently adjust classification error estimating structural relationships. package implements BCH- ML-based three-step estimators sandwich variance estimators propagate uncertainty measurement model classification-error correction process.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Unlike conventional latent class software uses one-step estimation approach, tseLCA allows measurement structural models estimated using different datasets. flexibility enables researchers calibrate measurement model primary reference sample subsequently apply resulting class definitions external dataset.

what: Support for Multiple Distal Outcome Types
title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: tseLCA provides native support broad range distal outcome distributions, including: Continuous outcomes (Gaussian) Count outcomes (Poisson) Binary outcomes (Bernoulli).

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Latent class estimation often susceptible local maxima convergence suboptimal solutions. improve estimation reliability, tseLCA incorporates automated diagnostic procedures monitor model quality measurement-model estimation.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: Following similar approach multilevLCA, tseLCA employs Full-Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML) estimation accommodate partially observed response patterns without discarding incomplete observations.

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: can install development version tseLCA GitHub like : tseLCA CRAN, can simply install CRAN server. read introductory vignette package’s webpage : https://SamLeeBYU.github.io/tseLCA/articles/tseLCA-workflow.html
code: # Install developmental tseLCA from the GitHub repository if (!require("pak")) {   install.packages("pak") }  pak::pak("SamLeeBYU/tseLCA") # Install tseLCA from CRAN install.packages("tseLCA")

title: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis
text: basic example shows simulate data run three-step LCA covariate single function call:
code: library(tseLCA)  # 1. Generate synthetic data  # (3 classes, 6 dichotomous items, and a multinomial logit covariate 'Zp') d <- generate_data(   n = 500,    separation = "high",    scenario = "covariate",    seed = 1 )  # 2. Estimate the three-step model # This automatically fits the measurement model and estimates covariate effects fit <- three_step(   data = d,   Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6),   n_classes = 3,   Zp.names = "Zp",   # Proportional assignment is recommended for better uncertainty propagation   use.modal.assignment = FALSE )  # 3. View the measurement and structural model estimates summary(fit)

three-step estimates #>                  C2         C3 #> Intercept  2.233384 -3.2742157 #> Zp        -1.156988  0.9400712 coef(fit, which = "two_step")  # two-step starting values #>                  C2         C3 #> Intercept  1.988800 -3.1317130 #> Zp        -1.017498  0.9190021 # title: Estimate covariate effects with measurement parameters fixed (two-step EM) — fitZ_from_fit0
text: fit0 Output lca_step1()$fit0. data data.frame. Y.names Character vector item column names. Zp.names Character vector covariate column names. tol Convergence tolerance. Default 1e-6. maxIter Maximum EM iterations. Default 200. incomplete Logical. FIML partially missing indicators. See Missing Data section vignette("tseLCA", package = "tseLCA"). Default FALSE. include.intercept Logical. Prepend intercept covariate design matrix. Default TRUE. rebase Character integer. Reference class multinomial logit parameterization (e.g. "C1", "C2", integer). Default "C1". Must match rebase used lca_step1() class column ordering consistent. starting_val Optional Q x (T-1) starting value matrix mGamma. verbose Logical. Print convergence messages. Default FALSE.

title: Estimate covariate effects with measurement parameters fixed (two-step EM) — fitZ_from_fit0
text: list following elements: mGamma Q x (T-1) numeric matrix multinomial logit coefficients, Q number columns covariate design matrix (including intercept include.intercept = TRUE). Rows named covariate, columns non-reference class (e.g. "C2", "C3"). mPhi Expanded item parameter matrix (items x classes), fixed fit0$mPhi throughout estimation. vOmega Length-T vector marginal class proportions implied final mGamma, computed column means fitted class probability matrix. LLKSeries Single-column matrix observed-data log-likelihoods, one row per EM iteration. Useful diagnosing convergence. converged Logical. TRUE EM loop exited maxIter iterations final log-likelihood change tol. n_obs Integer. Number observations used estimation listwise deletion covariates.

title: Estimate covariate effects with measurement parameters fixed (two-step EM) — fitZ_from_fit0
code: # \donttest{ d  <- generate_data(200, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) s1 <- lca_step1(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3)  # Estimate two-step gamma with mPhi fixed at Step-1 values fZ <- fitZ_from_fit0(   fit0     = s1$fit0,   data     = d,   Y.names  = paste0("Y", 1:6),   Zp.names = "Zp",   verbose  = TRUE ) #> fitZ EM converged in 9 iterations. fZ$mGamma   # Q x (T-1) coefficient matrix #>                  C2         C3 #> Intercept  1.988800 -3.1317130 #> Zp        -1.017498  0.9190021 fZ$converged #> [1] TRUE # }

title: Individual-level BHHH variance matrix for binary and polytomous LCA — lca_indiv_varmat
text: Computes outer-product (BHHH) information matrix variance-covariance matrix LCA measurement model parameters unconstrained (logit/log-ratio) space, matching multilevLCA's $Varmat.

title: Individual-level BHHH variance matrix for binary and polytomous LCA — lca_indiv_varmat
code: lca_indiv_varmat(   Y.exp,   mDesign.exp,   fit0,   ivItemcat,   boundary.tol = 0.01,   use.freq = TRUE,   u_post = NULL )

title: Individual-level BHHH variance matrix for binary and polytomous LCA — lca_indiv_varmat
text: Y.exp N x sum(K_h) expanded one-hot indicator matrix. mDesign.exp Expanded design matrix (dimensions Y.exp), NULL complete data. fit0 Step-1 fit object $vPi $mPhi. ivItemcat Integer vector category counts per item. boundary.tol Scalar tolerance boundary detection. Default 1e-2. use.freq Logical. Collapse duplicate score rows computing cross-product, weighting frequency. Default TRUE. u_post Optional N x T matrix posterior class probabilities. supplied (e.g. extracted fit0$mU via extract_Y_from_mU), compute_posteriors skipped. Default NULL.

title: Individual-level BHHH variance matrix for binary and polytomous LCA — lca_indiv_varmat
text: list following elements: Infomat Square BHHH information matrix dimension p x p, p = (T-1) + sum(ivItemcat - 1) * T total number free parameters. Boundary parameters zero rows columns. Varmat Inverse Infomat divided N, giving asymptotic variance-covariance matrix scale multilevLCA's $Varmat. Boundary parameters zero rows columns. SEs Numeric vector length p. Square root diagonal Varmat; zero boundary parameters. mScore N x p matrix individual score contributions unconstrained parameterization, used sandwich variance propagation lca_vcov lca_vcov_distal.

title: Individual-level BHHH variance matrix for binary and polytomous LCA — lca_indiv_varmat
text: score unconstrained space \(s_{} = u_{}(y_i - d_i \circ p_{})\), \(d_i\) missing-data design indicator matrix. Assumes fit0$mPhi follows multilevLCA storage convention: Dichotomous item h (ivItemcat[h] == 2): 1 row = \(P(Y=1|C)\); base level \(P(Y=0|C)\) excluded. Polytomous item h (ivItemcat[h] > 2): K_h rows = \(P(Y=0|C), \ldots, P(Y=K_h-1|C)\); base level included. expand_Y produces one-hot columns order expand_Phi(fit0$mPhi, ivItemcat) aligns column-wise expand_Y(, ivItemcat).  Free (estimable) parameters per item single \(P(Y=1|C)\) row dichotomous items, rows 2 \(K_h\) polytomous items (row 1, \(P(Y=0|C)\), reference). Boundary parameters (within boundary.tol 0 1) treated fixed: score columns zeroed contribute information matrix.

what: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
text: Fits three-step latent class model following steps: Measurement model: estimates latent class parameters (\(\pi\), \(\phi\)) using multilevLCA (Lyrvall et al., 2025). Classification-error matrix: computes posterior class probabilities T x T misclassification probability matrix \(P(W = s \mid X = t)\), standard errors corrected classification-error propagation (Bakk, Oberski & Vermunt, 2014). Structural model: estimates covariate effects using two-step starting values (Bakk & Kuha, 2018) /distal outcome means following Bakk, Tekle & Vermunt (2013), ML correction (Vermunt, 2010) BCH correction (Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars, 2004). See vignette("tseLCA", package = "tseLCA") worked example.

title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
code: three_step(   data,   Y.names,   n_classes,   Zp.names = NULL,   Zo.name = NULL,   step1 = NULL,   use.two.step = TRUE,   use.modal.assignment = TRUE,   include.intercept = TRUE,   use.simple.cov = FALSE,   incomplete = FALSE,   boundary.tol = 0.01,   maxIter.measurement = 5000,   measurement.tol = 1e-08,   covariate.tol = 1e-06,   iter.measurement = 10L,   R2.threshold = 0.7,   use.bch = FALSE,   em.maxIter = 200L,   get.twostep.vcov = FALSE,   rebase = "C1",   family = "gaussian",   correct.spec = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
text: data data.frame containing columns referenced Y.names, Zp.names, Zo.name. Y.names Character vector indicator column names. Need coded consecutive integers base level starting 0. n_classes Integer. Number latent classes. Zp.names Character vector covariate column names, NULL measurement-fit. Default NULL. Zo.name Single character name distal outcome column, NULL. Default NULL. step1 Pre-fitted Step-1 object (output lca_step1() prior three_step() call), NULL run Step 1 internally. Default NULL. use.two.step Logical. Initialize Step-3 two-step estimates. Default TRUE. use.modal.assignment Logical. Use modal (hard) class assignments Step 2 3. FALSE uses soft posterior weights. Default TRUE. include.intercept Logical. Prepend intercept column covariate design matrix. Default TRUE. use.simple.cov Logical. Skip Step-1 measurement-uncertainty correction return robust sandwich variance. Faster underestimates standard errors class separation low. Default FALSE. incomplete Logical. FIML partially missing indicators. See Missing Data section vignette("tseLCA", package = "tseLCA"). Default FALSE. boundary.tol Scalar. Parameters within tolerance 0 1 treated fixed computing Step-1 variance matrix numerical stability. Default 1e-2. maxIter.measurement Integer. Maximum EM iterations Step 1. Default 5000L. measurement.tol Scalar. Convergence tolerance Step-1 EM algorithm. Default 1e-8. covariate.tol Scalar. Convergence tolerance Step-3 Newton-Raphson EM algorithm. Default 1e-6. iter.measurement Integer. Number random restarts triggered Step-1 entropy R\(^2\) falls R2.threshold. Default 10L. R2.threshold Scalar. Entropy R\(^2\) threshold Step-1 random restarts triggered. Default 0.70. use.bch Logical. Use BCH-corrected weights instead ML estimator Step 3. May error BCH weights induce non-positive semi-definite Hessian third step (common cases low separation). Default FALSE. em.maxIter Integer. Maximum EM iterations Step-3 covariate distal outcome model. Default 200L. get.twostep.vcov Logical. TRUE, obtain multilevLCA's bias-corrected variance-covariance matrix two-step gamma estimates store $two_step_vcov. fitZ object passed via step1 already contains Varmat_cor (prior fitZ_from_multiLCA() plain multiLCA call), attached automatically even get.twostep.vcov = FALSE. Default FALSE. rebase Character (e.g. "C1", "C2") integer specifying latent class use reference category multinomial logit. measurement model permuted class becomes column 1 structural estimation. Default "C1". family Character. Distal outcome family: one "gaussian" (class means), "poisson" (log-rates), "binomial" (logits). Default "gaussian". correct.spec Logical. Use model-robust outer-product Hessian Step-3 standard errors rather observed-data Hessian. appropriate Step-3 model may misspecified. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print convergence messages. Default FALSE.

title: Three-step LCA estimation with covariates and/or distal outcomes — three_step
text: S3 object class tseLCA. subclass depends models estimated: tseLCA_measurement Returned neither Zp.names Zo.name supplied. Contains following elements: measurement_model Step-1 output list lca_step1(). llik Final Step-1 log-likelihood. AIC, BIC Information criteria measurement model. R2entr Entropy R\(^2\) measurement model. n_classes Number latent classes. posteriors N x T matrix soft posterior class probabilities. classifications Length-N integer vector modal class assignments. tseLCA_covariate Returned Zp.names supplied Zo.name NULL. Contains elements tseLCA_measurement plus: three_step Q x (T-1) matrix Step-3 gamma coefficients. three_step_vcov Q(T-1) x Q(T-1) variance-covariance matrix three_step, measurement-uncertainty correction unless use.simple.cov = TRUE. two_step Q x (T-1) matrix two-step starting values, NULL use.two.step = FALSE. two_step_vcov multilevLCA bias-corrected vcov two-step estimates, NULL. estimator Character: "ML" "BCH". entropy.R2 Covariate-adjusted entropy R\(^2\). llik Profile log-likelihood \(\sum_i \log \sum_t P(X=t|Z_{p,};\hat{\gamma}) P(Y_i|X=t;\hat{\phi})\), Step-1 parameters \(\hat{\phi}\) held fixed. construction smaller equivalent one-step MLE likelihood. tseLCA_distal Returned Zo.name supplied Zp.names NULL. Contains: three_step Named length-T vector Step-3 distal outcome parameters (means, log-rates, logits depending family). three_step_vcov T x T variance-covariance matrix three_step, named mu_C1 mu_CT. three_step.llik Step-3 distal log-likelihood \(\log P(Z_o|X=t)\) converged estimates. llik Profile log-likelihood \(\sum_i \log \sum_t P(X=t|\hat{\pi}) P(Z_{o,}|X=t;\hat{\mu}) P(Y_i|X=t;\hat{\phi})\), Step-1 parameters \(\hat{\pi}, \hat{\phi}\) held fixed. construction smaller equivalent one-step MLE likelihood. AIC Akaike information criterion based llik. BIC Bayesian information criterion based llik, using number distal-complete observations. family Character. distal outcome family used. estimator Character: "ML" "BCH". posteriors N x T soft posterior matrix. classifications Length-N modal class assignment vector. tseLCA_both Returned Zp.names Zo.name supplied. Contains: covariate tseLCA_covariate-structured sub-list (see ), including llik, AIC, BIC, entropy.R2. distal tseLCA_distal-structured sub-list (see ), including llik, AIC, BIC, three_step.llik. family, n_classes, estimator Shared top-level fields. posteriors, classifications Shared N x T posterior matrix length-N modal class vector.

BCH estimator fit_bch <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                       Zp.names = "Zp", use.bch = TRUE,                       use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit_bch) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : BCH #> Log-likelihood : -548.8676 #> AIC            : 1177.7352 #> BIC            : 1309.6679 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8595  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.9888 -3.1317 #> Zp        -1.0175  0.9190 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   2.4128    0.7552  3.1950 0.0014  **  #> Zp:C2         -1.2468    0.4043 -3.0837 0.0020  **  #> Intercept:C3  -2.9675    0.7342 -4.0419 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          0.8684    0.1952  4.4494 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Change reference class fit_c2 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                      Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = TRUE,                      rebase = "C2") summary(fit_c2) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -548.6403 #> AIC            : 1177.2805 #> BIC            : 1309.2132 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8589  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C1      C3 #> Intercept -1.9890 -5.1203 #> Zp         1.0175  1.9363 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C1  -2.2334    0.6258 -3.5688 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C1          1.1570    0.3002  3.8545 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -5.5076    0.9516 -5.7878 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          2.0971    0.3614  5.8019 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Gaussian distal outcome d2 <- generate_data(200, "high", "distal", seed = 2) fit_dis <- three_step(d2, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                       Zo.name = "Zo", family = "gaussian",                       use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit_dis) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Distal Outcome Model ------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Family         : gaussian #> Log-likelihood : -892.7558 #> AIC            : 1831.5116 #> BIC            : 1907.3729 #>  #> Distal outcome estimates by class: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> mu_C1 (mean)  -0.8223    0.1169 -7.0356 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C2 (mean)   1.0946    0.1141  9.5956 < 0.001 *** #> mu_C3 (mean)   0.0492    0.1531  0.3212 0.7480      #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # Pass a pre-fitted measurement model to skip Step 1 fit_step1 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3) fit2 <- three_step(d, Y.names = paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                    Zp.names = "Zp", step1 = fit_step1,                    use.simple.cov = TRUE) summary(fit2) #> -- tseLCA Three-Step Covariate Model ----------------------- #> Latent classes : 3 #> Estimator      : ML #> Log-likelihood : -548.6403 #> AIC            : 1177.2805 #> BIC            : 1309.2132 #> Entropy R²     : 0.8589  (covariate-adjusted) #>  #> Two-step (starting) estimates: #>                C2      C3 #> Intercept  1.9888 -3.1317 #> Zp        -1.0175  0.9190 #>  #> Three-step estimates: #>              Estimate Std.Error z.value     p.value #> Intercept:C2   2.2334    0.6258  3.5688 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C2         -1.1570    0.3002 -3.8545 < 0.001 *** #> Intercept:C3  -3.2742    0.7191 -4.5529 < 0.001 *** #> Zp:C3          0.9401    0.1896  4.9587 < 0.001 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1  # what: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: tseLCA implements bias-adjusted three-step estimators structural latent class models covariates distal outcomes. Building efficient measurement-model estimation multilevLCA (Lyrvall et al., 2025), tseLCA adds modern three-step estimators, classification-error corrections, analytic sandwich variance estimation propagates measurement uncertainty latent class measurement stage final structural parameter estimates. contrast one-step approaches poLCA, including covariates may alter underlying latent class definitions, three-step methods fix measurement model estimating structural relationships adjust classification error final stage. tseLCA also allows measurement structural models estimated different datasets, enabling researchers calibrate measurement model large reference sample apply separate analysis sample.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Measurement model: estimate class-conditional item-response probabilities \(\phi\) class prevalences \(\pi\) using multilevLCA (Lyrvall et al., 2025 ). Classification-error matrix: assign posterior class probabilities compute T x T misclassification matrix \(P(W = s \mid X = t)\), standard errors corrected classification-error propagation (Bakk, Oberski & Vermunt, 2014). Structural model: estimate covariate effects using two-step starting values (Bakk & Kuha, 2018) /distal outcome (Bakk, Tekle & Vermunt) means either ML correction (Vermunt, 2010) BCH correction (Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars, 2004).

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: three_step Full three-step estimation pipeline. Accepts covariates (Zp.names), distal outcomes (Zo.name), . Handles Steps 1–3 single call, optional pre-fitted Step-1 input step1. lca_step1 Standalone Step-1 measurement model estimation via multilevLCA. Returns reusable fit object can passed three_step() avoid re-estimating measurement model across multiple structural specifications. fitZ_from_fit0 Two-step covariate estimation fixing measurement parameters Step-1 values estimating multinomial logit coefficients \(\gamma\) EM algorithm. Returns starting values Step 3. Custom starting values can supplied via starting_val. fitZ_from_multiLCA Two-step covariate estimation via multiLCA(fixedpars = 1), returning multilevLCA's bias-corrected standard errors. Called automatically get.twostep.vcov = TRUE three_step. generate_data Simulate data replicating Bakk & Kuha (2018) three-class design: six binary indicators across three separation levels ("low", "medium", "high") two scenarios ("covariate" "distal").

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: ML (default, use.bch = FALSE) Vermunt (2010) ML correction uses weighted pseudo-likelihood misclassification matrix bias adjustment. Preferred class separation low moderate. BCH (use.bch = TRUE) Bolck, Croon & Hagenaars (2004) correction inverts misclassification matrix obtain direct class weights. Works well high separation may produce ill-conditioned Hessian (non-positive semi-definite covariance matrix) separation low; use ML estimator case.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Full correction (use.simple.cov = FALSE, default) Analytic propagation Step-1 measurement uncertainty classification-error correction, following Bakk, Oberski & Vermunt (2014). Uses soft (proportional) posteriors Jacobian \(\partial\theta_2/\partial\theta_1\) regardless use.modal.assignment. Recommended separation moderate low. Simple/robust (use.simple.cov = TRUE) Sandwich SEs Step 3 , ignoring measurement uncertainty. useful computational shortcut separation high correction negligible.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Modal (use.modal.assignment = TRUE, default) observation assigned probable class (hard assignment). Jacobian variance correction still computed soft posteriors. Proportional (use.modal.assignment = FALSE) Soft posterior weights used throughout Steps 2 3. Recommended separation moderate low, required mathematically well-defined analytic Jacobian.

title: tseLCA: Three-Step Estimation for Latent Class Analysis — tseLCA-package
text: Binary polytomous indicators, following multilevLCA coding conventions. Gaussian, Poisson, binomial distal outcome families. Full-information maximum likelihood (FIML) partially observed indicator patterns (incomplete = TRUE). Step 3 always performs listwise deletion missing covariates distal outcomes. Flexible measurement structural samples: fit measurement model reference sample apply different analysis sample via step1 argument. Arbitrary reference class multinomial logit parameterization via rebase argument. Log-likelihoods invariant choice. Joint covariate distal outcome estimation (Zp.names Zo.name supplied together). covariate model estimated first; covariate-adjusted posteriors used priors distal outcome step. S3 methods (print, summary, coef, vcov, plot) four return subclasses: tseLCA_measurement, tseLCA_covariate, tseLCA_distal, tseLCA_both.

NOT probabilities" # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zp.names = "Zp", use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit)   # Q*(T-1) x Q*(T-1) vcov matrix with named rows/cols #>              Intercept:C2        Zp:C2 Intercept:C3        Zp:C3 #> Intercept:C2  0.391644881 -0.173583653  0.001643327 -0.002599746 #> Zp:C2        -0.173583653  0.090099886  0.016315352 -0.002300251 #> Intercept:C3  0.001643327  0.016315352  0.517169347 -0.130664301 #> Zp:C3        -0.002599746 -0.002300251 -0.130664301  0.035941355 # } # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "distal", seed = 2) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zo.name = "Zo", use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit)   # T x T vcov matrix with mu_C1..mu_CT row/col names #>               mu_C1         mu_C2         mu_C3 #> mu_C1  1.365920e-02  5.055316e-05 -8.311167e-05 #> mu_C2  5.055316e-05  1.301282e-02 -9.122807e-04 #> mu_C3 -8.311167e-05 -9.122807e-04  2.342768e-02 # } # \donttest{ d   <- generate_data(200, "high", "covariate", seed = 1) d$Zo <- rnorm(200, mean = c(-1, 0, 1)[d$X], sd = 0.5) fit <- three_step(d, paste0("Y", 1:6), n_classes = 3,                   Zp.names = "Zp", Zo.name = "Zo",                   use.simple.cov = TRUE) vcov(fit, which = "covariate") #>              Intercept:C2        Zp:C2 Intercept:C3        Zp:C3 #> Intercept:C2  0.391644881 -0.173583653  0.001643327 -0.002599746 #> Zp:C2        -0.173583653  0.090099886  0.016315352 -0.002300251 #> Intercept:C3  0.001643327  0.016315352  0.517169347 -0.130664301 #> Zp:C3        -0.002599746 -0.002300251 -0.130664301  0.035941355 vcov(fit, which = "distal") #>               mu_C1         mu_C2         mu_C3 #> mu_C1  3.772208e-03  3.058231e-05 -1.253885e-05 #> mu_C2  3.058231e-05  5.147141e-03 -6.123296e-06 #> mu_C3 -1.253885e-05 -6.123296e-06  3.590917e-03 # }

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Initial submission CRAN.

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Implemented BCH ML bias-adjusted three-step estimators latent class analysis (LCA). Added support structural models containing covariates (ZpZ_p), distal outcomes (ZoZ_o), combined models (estimating relationship ZpZ_p latent class first, followed distal outcome adjusting covariate-adjusted posteriors). Implemented analytic sandwich variance estimation correctly propagate measurement uncertainty first-step LCA classification-error correction final step. Added robust standard error option (use.simple.cov = TRUE) bypasses measurement-uncertainty correction faster computation large, well-separated samples.

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Integrated ‘multilevLCA’ package efficient Step-1 measurement model estimation. Added support polytomous indicator items (0-based integer coding). Implemented Full Information Maximum Likelihood (FIML) handle missing data measurement model via incomplete = TRUE argument (using two-pass row-filtering strategy). Added ability pass pre-fitted measurement model (via step1 argument) reuse across multiple structural models apply different sample subsets. Implemented automated random restarts measurement model triggered entropy R2R^2 falls user-specified threshold.

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Added support modal proportional (soft) posterior class assignment (use.modal.assignment). Integrated Gaussian, Poisson, binomial families distal outcome estimation. Added rebase argument allow users easily change reference latent class multinomial logit parameterization maintaining invariant log-likelihoods. Implemented two-step EM estimation (fitZ_from_fit0()) generate stable starting values three-step structural model.

title: tseLCA 1.0.0
text: Included standard S3 methods tseLCA objects: summary(), coef(), vcov(), plot() (delegates ‘multilevLCA’ item-profile visualization). Built data-generating process (generate_data()) replicates Bakk & Kuha (2018) simulation study design covariates distal outcomes varying separation conditions.
